# Copilot Instructions - V-Lesson Plus

## Project Context

- Chrome Extension Manifest V3.
- Target site: https://www.uninettunouniversity.net/*
- Main goal: improve videolesson playback UX (speed control, resume/marks roadmap).

## Architecture

- Source code in `src/` (TypeScript).
- Runtime bundles in `dist/` generated by esbuild scripts.
- Popup UI logic in `src/popup.ts`.
- Page interaction in `src/content.ts`.
- Shared utilities in `src/logic.ts`.

## Build and Test

- Install: `npm i`
- Build: `npm run build`
- Tests: `npm test`
- Keep build green before committing.
- After every change, run both `npm run build` and `npm test` before considering the task done.

## Coding Best Practices

- Keep functions small and explicit.
- Prefer strict typing over `any`; use narrow interfaces for messages.
- Do not introduce breaking behavior in popup/content message contracts without updating both sides.
- Keep constants centralized for repeated values (timeouts, selectors, allowed speeds).
- Add defensive checks for missing tab/video/document state.
- Log meaningful messages with `[V-Lesson Plus]` prefix.

## UX Best Practices

- Popup should degrade gracefully when no supported video is present.
- Any action should return visible feedback (status text or in-page toast).
- Keep UI simple and fast; avoid heavy DOM manipulations.

## Storage and Data

- Use `chrome.storage.local` for playback progress/marks.
- Normalize keys (URL/video identity) before storing.
- Avoid high-frequency writes; throttle or interval-save.

## Testing Best Practices

- Add/update unit tests for shared logic whenever behavior changes.
- Validate normalization helpers and edge cases.
- Prefer deterministic tests with document-like stubs.

## Manifest and Permissions

- Keep permissions minimal.
- If adding features requiring permissions (e.g., storage), update manifest and document why.
- Keep icons and asset paths consistent with folder structure.

## Git and PR Hygiene

- Do not commit `node_modules/` or generated artifacts unless intentionally required.
- Keep commits small and focused.
- Include a short verification checklist in PR descriptions (build + test + manual extension check).
